channel.title: EMERGENZ Biosecurity Intelligence Dashboard
channel.description: High-severity biosecurity signals and recent authority/media coverage. Generated 2026-05-31T13:59:58.438Z.

item.title: [ACTION] Ebola disease — Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo
item.description: WHO has declared the Bundibugyo ebolavirus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with Africa CDC confirming active outbreak monitoring in both the DRC and Uganda. Per WHO Disease Outbreak News (29 May 2026), the DRC has reported 125 confirmed cases (17 deaths) and 906 suspected cases (223 suspected deaths, as of 27 May), and Uganda has reported 9 confirmed cases (1 death). Multi-health-zone spread in eastern DRC continues; cross-border movement elevates geographic risk.

item.title: [CONCERN] Andes virus hantavirus cluster — MV Hondius travel-linked
item.description: Andes hantavirus cluster linked to a South America–Antarctica expedition cruise aboard MV Hondius. 13 reported cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths across multiple countries, with multinational case-finding and post-exposure monitoring continuing.

item.title: [CONCERN] Measles — United States
item.description: CDC confirmed 1,983 measles cases across 40 jurisdictions in 2026 (as of May 28), representing 30 outbreaks with 93% of cases outbreak-associated. No deaths reported in 2026. U.S. kindergartener MMR coverage has declined to 92.5% (from 95.2% in 2019–2020), leaving approximately 286,000 kindergarteners unvaccinated — the structural driver of sustained outbreak activity.

item.title: [CONCERN] Mpox — clade I, Africa & travel-linked importations
item.description: Mpox clade I and travel-linked importation monitoring remain operationally significant for clinical recognition, specimen routing, and laboratory clade differentiation. Verify current country-level trends and any importation details against specific Africa CDC, WHO, or CDC updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Cholera — sub-Saharan Africa, including Republic of Congo
item.description: Africa CDC reported 40,707 cholera cases continent-wide from January 1 through May 14, 2026 — a 65% year-over-year decline compared to the same period in 2025. Despite the overall volume decrease, case fatality ratios remain elevated in conflict-affected and low-healthcare-access settings. Sudan, Ethiopia, Somalia, Mozambique, Malawi, and Zimbabwe continue reporting active transmission.

item.title: [CONCERN] Chikungunya — global outbreak activity
item.description: Chikungunya surveillance remains active across parts of the Americas and other mosquito-active regions. Verify current country burden and case totals against specific PAHO or WHO updates before publication.

item.title: Google News: Ebola Outbreak: WHO Chief Says Five Cases Ended In Recovery (Live Updates) - Forbes
item.description: Ebola Outbreak: WHO Chief Says Five Cases Ended In Recovery (Live Updates) Forbes

item.title: Google News: Congo and Uganda report 263 confirmed Ebola cases with 43 deaths, Africa CDC says - Reuters
item.description: Congo and Uganda report 263 confirmed Ebola cases with 43 deaths, Africa CDC says Reuters

item.title: Google News: Video WHO chief arrives at Ebola epicenter - ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
item.description: Video WHO chief arrives at Ebola epicenter ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

item.title: NPR: Experimental pill promises new hope for deadly pancreatic cancer
item.description: A novel pill helped people with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer, researchers reported Sunday, raising hopes of long-needed better treatments for one of the deadliest types of cancer.

item.title: Google News: Two more children die of measles-like symptoms - The Daily Star
item.description: Two more children die of measles-like symptoms The Daily Star

item.title: Google News: Mpox outbreak confirmed in Embu County as health officials intensify response - Citizen Digital
item.description: Mpox outbreak confirmed in Embu County as health officials intensify response Citizen Digital

item.title: Google News: 5 Ebola patients have recovered from rare strain of virus, WHO chief says - CBS News
item.description: 5 Ebola patients have recovered from rare strain of virus, WHO chief says CBS News

item.title: Google News: Wisconsin reports 1st mpox cases of the year - Outbreak News Today
item.description: Wisconsin reports 1st mpox cases of the year Outbreak News Today

item.title: NPR: The biggest permanent desert lake threatens with rising waters and hungry crocs
item.description: Kenya's Lake Turkana is the world's largest permanent desert lake. Its waters have long sustained hundreds of thousands. Now the lake is facing multiple threats — and threatening those who rely on it.

item.title: Google News: Nigeria: Cholera outbreak in Borno State - Outbreak News Today
item.description: Nigeria: Cholera outbreak in Borno State Outbreak News Today

item.title: Google News: Africa’s response to Ebola must be defined by Africa itself - Financial Times
item.description: Africa’s response to Ebola must be defined by Africa itself Financial Times

item.title: NPR: Amid a slew of chemical accidents, the agency that investigates them is under threat
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks with former member of the U.S. Chemical Safety Board Rick Engler about the current state of chemical safety following a string of incidents in the U.S.

item.title: NPR: Pancreatic cancer was once considered almost untreatable. A new drug is offering hope
item.description: A new drug for pancreatic cancer gives some hope for one of the most dire types of cancer.

item.title: Google News: Hantavirus, ebola and other pandemics yet to come | Pius Kamau - Denver Gazette
item.description: Hantavirus, ebola and other pandemics yet to come | Pius Kamau Denver Gazette

item.title: STAT News: STAT+: For prostate cancer patients set on surgery, new hormone regimen may improve outcomes, study finds
item.description: By doing hormone therapy up front in prostate cancer cases, the hope was to shrink the relapse rate after surgery. Results were encouraging.

item.title: STAT News: STAT+: Akeso and Summit’s ivonescimab extends survival in squamous cell lung cancer
item.description: A lung cancer drug developed in China was a highlight of ASCO but doctors want to see follow-up in a more diverse population.

item.title: STAT News: STAT+: Practice-changing results reported for Revolution Medicines pancreatic cancer drug
item.description: Much-awaited results at ASCO show that scientists have indeed found a way to drug a "greasy ball" involved in pancreatic cancer.

item.title: NBC News: After new drug’s ‘unprecedented’ results for pancreatic cancer, doctors look at other uses
item.description: The experimental drug daraxonrasib, which doubled survival time in patients with advanced pancreatic cancer, may also prove effective for lung, colon and ovarian cancers.

item.title: Science News: A new pancreatic cancer pill may be a game changer for patients
item.description: Daraxonrasib, which nearly doubled patients' survival time, fights the disease in a new way. It bear-hugs a cancer protein that drives cell growth.

item.title: Google News: Measles outbreak: Death toll rises to 585 with two more deaths in 24 hrs - The Business Standard
item.description: Measles outbreak: Death toll rises to 585 with two more deaths in 24 hrs The Business Standard
